summary: China pursues a sovereign path through the State Cryptography Administration (OSCCA), which funds anti-quantum cryptography as a priority research direction of its National Cryptography Science Fund. The associated standards institute is running a national solicitation for next-generation commercial cryptographic algorithms expressly to address the quantum computing threat and to set China's own standards.
govActors:
OSCCA | State Cryptography Administration, funds anti-quantum cryptography research through the National Cryptography Science Fund
ICCS (Commercial Cryptography Standards Research Institute) | Runs the solicitation for China's next-generation commercial cryptographic algorithms against the quantum threat
provenance: Lead institution + Sovereign process: OSCCA Announcement No. 55 (ingested, 2026) names 'anti-quantum computing cryptography key theory and technology' as the first research direction of the National Cryptography Science Fund; OSCCA is China's national cryptography authority running its own scheme, hence Sovereign

Next-generation algorithm solicitation: ICCS announcements and proposal-solicitation notices (candidates), framed as a response to quantum computing threats and aimed at China's own standardisation - REVIEW (these ICCS rows are included:false, used here only as raw description context)
No PQC algorithm names, standard families, hybrid stance, dated milestones or target completion stated in the ingested document - all omitted per iron rule
CAICT 2023 report references NIST PQC progress but is a survey (included:false) and does not make NIST China's posture
Model 2026-06-14 (sovereign-bloc / sovereign-developer, High): SM-series mandated under the 2020 Cryptography Law; NGCC programme (Feb 2025) building sovereign PQC independent of NIST.
classificationBasis:
Lead institution + Sovereign process: OSCCA Announcement No. 55 (ingested, 2026) names 'anti-quantum computing cryptography key theory and technology' as the first research direction of the National Cryptography Science Fund; OSCCA is China's national cryptography authority running its own scheme, hence Sovereign
Next-generation algorithm solicitation: ICCS announcements and proposal-solicitation notices (candidates), framed as a response to quantum computing threats and aimed at China's own standardisation - REVIEW (these ICCS rows are included:false, used here only as raw description context)
No PQC algorithm names, standard families, hybrid stance, dated milestones or target completion stated in the ingested document - all omitted per iron rule
CAICT 2023 report references NIST PQC progress but is a survey (included:false) and does not make NIST China's posture
mainRegulation:
Cryptography Law of the PRC (2020) | national | binding-law
SM-series (SM2/SM3/SM4/SM9) commercial cryptography standards | national | binding-law
Multi-Level Protection Scheme, level 3+ domestic algorithms | national | binding-law
NGCC programme for sovereign PQC standards (2025) | national | guidance
obligation: Critical information infrastructure operators must use domestic (SM-series) algorithms under the Cryptography Law and the Multi-Level Protection Scheme; sovereign post-quantum standards are in development through the NGCC programme and not yet mandated.
